title: centaurea scabiosa subsp. alpina
intro: Centaurea scabiosa subsp. alpina, also known as alpine scabious, is a flowering plant species belonging to the daisy family, characterized by its delicate purple flowers and a height range of 30-60 centimeters. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been utilized in the treatment of various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Centaurea scabiosa subsp. alpina include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
